rulings.Case Title: ROYOCO CONSTRUCTION VS. FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 8, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANT FAR EAST NATIONAL BANK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the first cause of action (Breach of contract) for plaintiff to clarify the "special instance and request" alleged in paragraph 12, and how Far East made the request to plaintiff, or haow the contract was assigned to Far East. Demurrer overruled as to the remaining causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NELSEN, LORENA; INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF VS. LEGACY PARTNERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf To Submit Her Claims To Individual Arbitration;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 8, 2010, line 8. DEFENDANT LEGACY PARTNERS RESIDENTIAL, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit Her Claims To Individual Arbitration. Granted. The court finds a valid arbitration agreement and the dispute is covered by the arbitration agreement. Plaintiff fails to establish a ground for denial of the motion. The agreement is not procedurally or substantively unconscionable. the agreement signed by plaintiff clearly addresses binding arbitration. The agreement is silent regarding class actions, and plaintiff does not demonstrate that the case falls within the rules established by Gentry v. Superior Court (2007) 42 CAL 4th 443, and Discover Bank v. Superior Court (2005) 36 CAL 4th 148. Strong public policy favors arbitration. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 8, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANTS SOPHIA SIMAS AND NOVA COMMERCIAL COMPANY, INC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. GRANTED with 10 days leave to amend to allege (1) that the malicious conduct exacerbated plaintiff's injuries; and (2) facts showing how the corporate employer is liable for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR CMLTI VS. WILLIAM P. FIRPO et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 8, 2010, line 10. DEFENDANTS LOTTIE TAYLOR and JASON TAYLOR'S MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. GRANTED. Defendants Lottie Taylor and Jason Talor demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, surprise , or excusable neglect pursuant to CCP 473. They acted diligently to set aside the default, and the law favors adjudication on the merits. =(302/CWW)
